info.title: SphereRabbitMQ IaC Tool
info.description: Command-line entry point for RabbitMQ topology infrastructure as code.

commands.name: apply
commands.description:
Apply a topology plan to RabbitMQ.
This command only performs safe reconciliation and refuses destructive plans unless --migrate is provided.
Example:
sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --dry-run
sprmq apply --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --migrate

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to the topology YAML file.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output format: text or json. [default: Text]

options.name: --management-url
options.description: RabbitMQ Management API URL.

options.name: --username
options.description: RabbitMQ username.

options.name: --password
options.description: RabbitMQ password.

options.name: --vhost
options.description: Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Compute and print the apply result without changing the broker. [default: False]

options.name: --migrate
options.description: Allow supported exchange and queue migrations when immutable broker arguments changed. [default: False]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: completion
commands.description:
Print shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, or pwsh.
Example:
sprmq completion bash

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: shell
arguments.description: Target shell: bash, zsh, or pwsh.

commands.name: destroy
commands.description:
Intentionally delete every virtual host declared in the YAML file.
Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to the topology YAML file.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output format: text or json. [default: Text]

options.name: --management-url
options.description: RabbitMQ Management API URL.

options.name: --username
options.description: RabbitMQ username.

options.name: --password
options.description: RabbitMQ password.

options.name: --vhost
options.description: Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Compute and print the apply result without changing the broker. [default: False]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]

options.name: --allow-destructive
options.description: Allow destructive execution for commands that delete broker resources. [default: False]

options.name: --auto-approve
options.description: Skip the interactive confirmation prompt for destructive commands. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: export
commands.description:
Export broker topology to YAML.
Example:
sprmq export --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml --output-file topology.yaml
sprmq export --include-broker --output-file topology.yaml

options.name: --file
options.description: Optional topology YAML file used as a source for broker connection settings.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output format: text or json. [default: Text]

options.name: --management-url
options.description: RabbitMQ Management API URL.

options.name: --username
options.description: RabbitMQ username.

options.name: --password
options.description: RabbitMQ password.

options.name: --vhost
options.description: Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.

options.name: --output-file
options.description: Output file path for export. Use '-' to write YAML to stdout. [default: -]

options.name: --include-broker
options.description: Include the resolved broker settings in the exported YAML. [default: False]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create a topology YAML file from a built-in template.
Example:
sprmq init --template retry-dead-letter --output-file topology.yaml

options.name: --template
options.description: Template name. Available: debug, minimal, quorum, retry, retry-dead-letter, topic-routing. [default: minimal]

options.name: --output-file
options.description: Output file path for init. Use '-' to write YAML to stdout. [default: -]

options.name: --force
options.description: Overwrite the output file when it already exists. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: plan
commands.description:
Compare desired topology against broker state and print a reconciliation plan.
This command never changes broker state.
Example:
sprmq plan --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to the topology YAML file.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output format: text or json. [default: Text]

options.name: --management-url
options.description: RabbitMQ Management API URL.

options.name: --username
options.description: RabbitMQ username.

options.name: --password
options.description: RabbitMQ password.

options.name: --vhost
options.description: Virtual host filter. Repeat to pass multiple values.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print detailed execution phases and broker operations. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: purge
commands.description:
Remove all messages from every queue declared by the normalized topology.
Generated retry, dead-letter, and debug queues are included when present in the YAML.
Non-dry execution requires --allow-destructive and asks for confirmation unless --auto-approve is used.

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Validate topology YAML syntax, normalization, and semantic consistency.
Example:
sprmq validate --file samples/minimal-topology.yaml
